success_surface: Cell-level formula insertion with condition-based logic across multiple rows
effective_behaviors: Using openpyxl for direct formula manipulation instead of pandas
Iterating through explicit row ranges to apply formulas consistently across target cells
Constructing Excel formulas with nested conditional logic (IF/SEARCH) for keyword detection
Preserving original source file by writing to separate output path
Ensuring output directory exists before file save operation
Using relative cell references in formulas for automatic row adjustment
Logging modified cell ranges and success status for verification
evidence: Used load_workbook() and ws['E{row}'] pattern to insert formulas as raw strings into cells E6:E13
Applied range(6, 14) loop to cover all 8 rows requiring formula insertion
Built nested IF(ISNUMBER(SEARCH(...))) formula structure for dual keyword matching
Created output directory with os.makedirs() before wb.save()
Saved to new output_path instead of modifying source file in-place
Printed confirmation message showing modified cells and output path

memory_items.title: Formula Range Iteration Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When applying formulas across multiple consecutive rows/columns, iterate through the exact range using integer indices to maintain consistent relative references.
memory_items.generalization: For multi-cell formula operations, loop through the target range programmatically rather than hardcoding individual cell assignments, using f-strings to build dynamic cell references.

memory_items.title: Condition-Based Formula Construction
memory_items.lesson: Complex cell content requirements are best addressed with nested Excel functions written as raw strings.
memory_items.generalization: When formula logic depends on detecting keywords or comparing values, construct nested IF/ISNUMBER/SEARCH combinations to handle multiple conditions while maintaining case-insensitive matching where appropriate.

memory_items.title: Output Safety Protocol
memory_items.lesson: File operations should ensure destination paths exist before attempting saves to prevent runtime errors.
memory_items.generalization: Always verify and create parent directories for output files before writing, and preserve the original workbook by directing output to a different location unless explicitly instructed otherwise.

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Application Patterns
- **Multi-Row Formulas**: When inserting formulas across multiple cells, use programmatic iteration through the target range (e.g., range(start_row, end_row + 1)) with f-string formatted cell references for consistency.
- **Conditional Logic**: For keyword-based or multi-condition formulas, nest IF statements with SEARCH/ISNUMBER functions to detect text patterns while preserving case-insensitivity.
- **Relative References**: Design formulas with relative references so they auto-adjust per row; avoid hardcoded absolute ranges within iterative formulas.
- **Directory Safety**: Always verify output directory exists (os.makedirs with exist_ok=True) before saving files to prevent path-related failures.
- **Source Protection**: Load source workbooks read-only conceptually; always save results to the specified output path rather than modifying the input file directly.
